response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience and strong software craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, mentoring), but the role explicitly requires a C++ developer with 8-12 years building high-performance, multi-threaded, secure industrial systems. C++ exposure is limited to a brief 2007-2008 stint at National Instruments roughly 18 years ago, and current work is TypeScript/Node/DLang focused. No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, industrial protocols, or message-broker architectures at scale. The skill mismatch on the core technical stack is too wide to justify a senior C++ role.
strengths: Long track record of low-latency, high-throughput backend work (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP req/s)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source authorship
Experience with distributed/event-driven systems and Node.js/REST APIs relevant to the advantageous skills list
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months at NI in 2007-2008); no recent STL, multi-threading, or modern C++ work shown
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No experience with MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or industrial automation domain — core to this role
